title: Raukaua simplex
intro: Raukaua simplex, also known as New Zealand horseradish, is a perennial plant native to the New Zealand region, belonging to the family Araliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues and digestive discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Raukaua simplex include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Raukaua simplex is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
